title: FileMiddleware path traversal: '..' checked before percent-decoding
category: path traversal (order-of-operations: directory-escape check runs before percent-decode)
crash_site: Vapor app serving static files via FileMiddleware returns files outside the configured public directory when the request path contains percent-encoded traversal sequences.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the percent-decoding call (removingPercentEncoding) and the file-read/NIOFileSystem access where the traversal lands. The defect is that respond performs the '..' containment check on the still-encoded path, then decodes.
ground_truth.fix_summary: FileMiddleware.respond checked for '..' on the raw (still percent-encoded) path and only decoded afterward, so an input like /%2e%2e/ passed the check but resolved to ../ -> path traversal. Fix: decode percent-encoding before the containment check (fix the relative percent decoding order).

admission.note: Authored from PR #2500 (CVE-2020-15230) body+diff. crash != cause: the traversal 'lands' at the file-read; the cause is FileMiddleware.respond doing the '..' check on the encoded path before decoding. Decoy = the decode call. Single-file Swift logic -> watch contamination/grep. Pure Swift-LANGUAGE instance. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/vapor/vapor/pull/2500 — fix relative percent decoding in file middleware (CVE-2020-15230); fix cf1651f7ff76, base 236c616ca1d7.
